title: Community rumor control board
summary: A low-tech method for separating confirmed facts, pending reports, rumors, and harmful claims during a village, building, camp, or family disruption.
content:
A low-tech method for separating confirmed facts, pending reports, rumors, and harmful claims during a village, building, camp, or family disruption.

Use it when the situation is real but still manageable. Work slowly, keep one written decision log, and prefer the safest reversible action over a dramatic improvisation.

Core sequence: 1) Create four columns: confirmed, checking, rumor, and action taken. 2) Write the source, time, exact words, and who is checking before repeating a report. 3) Move a claim only when a named person confirms it through an official channel, direct observation, or documented call. 4) Remove private names from public notices unless consent or immediate life safety requires disclosure.

Stop point: Do not use this card for community rumor control board to replace emergency services, medical prescriptions, utility professionals, or official evacuation orders. Main warnings: Do not treat community rumor control board as finished until the result is visible, dated, and understood by the next person. Do not continue community rumor control board when smell, heat, illness, violence, rising water, or official instructions indicate immediate danger.
materials: visible label sheet reserved for Community rumor control board
clean container or pouch dedicated to Community rumor control board
pencil log with date and responsible person for Community rumor control board
safe storage spot chosen for Community · Information control
steps: Create four columns: confirmed, checking, rumor, and action taken.
Write the source, time, exact words, and who is checking before repeating a report.
Move a claim only when a named person confirms it through an official channel, direct observation, or documented call.
Remove private names from public notices unless consent or immediate life safety requires disclosure.
warnings: Do not treat community rumor control board as finished until the result is visible, dated, and understood by the next person.
Do not continue community rumor control board when smell, heat, illness, violence, rising water, or official instructions indicate immediate danger.
common_mistakes: Keeping the plan for community rumor control board only in one person’s memory makes handover fail during fatigue.
Using unmarked supplies during community rumor control board causes accidental mixing, waste, or unfair distribution.
alternatives: If the full setup for community rumor control board is impossible, protect the highest-risk person or area first and record what remains undone.
Use a spoken read-back and a paper mark when devices or normal forms are unavailable for community rumor control board.
failure_signs: The same question about community rumor control board is repeated because nobody can see the current decision.
A person affected by community rumor control board is excluded from the plan or cannot explain the next safe step.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card for community rumor control board to replace emergency services, medical prescriptions, utility professionals, or official evacuation orders.
Do not hide private information collected for community rumor control board in a public place without clear need and consent.
short_term: Within the first operating period for community rumor control board, create a visible status mark, name one owner, and check whether the highest-risk person is safer than before.
long_term: After the disruption, review community rumor control board with the people who used it and replace unclear labels, weak storage choices, and steps that caused delay.
if_method_fails: If community rumor control board fails, pause the routine, isolate the unsafe part, document the failure point, and use the simplest safe fallback until qualified help or official guidance arrives.
environment_notes: Adapt community rumor control board for apartment stairs, villages, ferries, camps, heat, cold, smoke, water shortage, and disability access without removing the stop rule.
